summary: The server-rendered question, quiz, and attempt forms do not include any CSRF token or equivalent anti-CSRF validation. A malicious site can cause a logged-in victim to create or modify questions and quizzes, change quiz mappings, or start and submit attempts using the victim's existing session.

evidence_snippets.reason: The delete-quiz and start-attempt forms are state-changing POSTs with no CSRF token.
The question creation handler accepts cookie-authenticated browser POST requests without verifying any CSRF token.
The question update handler performs state changes using only cookie-based authentication.
The question deletion handler processes browser POST requests without request authenticity validation.
The quiz creation handler accepts authenticated browser POST requests without any anti-CSRF mechanism.
The quiz update handler accepts state-changing browser POST requests without validating request authenticity.
The quiz deletion handler is a forgeable POST.
The quiz-question mapping handler performs a privileged change without CSRF validation.
Student attempt lifecycle actions rely solely on the browser's authenticated cookies and do not verify a per-session anti-CSRF token.

root_cause: Server-rendered quiz and question workflows rely on cookie-based authentication but do not require a request authenticity token or equivalent CSRF defense for state-changing form submissions.
repair_plan: Introduce server-side CSRF protection for all browser quiz workflows by generating a per-session token, embedding it in every state-changing question, quiz, mapping, and attempt form, and rejecting requests whose token is missing or invalid. Apply the same protection consistently to create, update, delete, start, answer, and submit actions.
